pattern: Reducing each array to a set first turns 'present in at least two of the three arrays' into a simple pairwise-intersection union.
hints: Duplicates within a single array don't matter — convert each array to a set first.
A value belongs in the answer if it shows up in at least two of the three sets, which is the same as being in the union of every pairwise intersection.
Compute set1 & set2, set1 & set3, and set2 & set3, then union all three results together for the final answer.

edge_inputs.description: minimum size single-element arrays that all agree
no value appears in two or more arrays
duplicates within a single array must not be double counted
value present in exactly two of the three arrays
every value shared across all three arrays

stress.description: three arrays over a small shared value range to maximize overlaps
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    nums1 = [rng.randint(1, 20) for _ in range(n)]
    nums2 = [rng.randint(1, 20) for _ in range(n)]
    nums3 = [rng.randint(1, 20) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums1, nums2, nums3]
